checks.a_analysis: The HTML output successfully preserves the linear organizational structure of the source presentation. Content is grouped into logical sections that correspond to the original slides, with headings correctly representing slide titles. Although the presentation's visual complexity is significantly diminished by missing graphical elements (addressed in Check V3), the structural flow and placement of textual information and tables remain faithful to the source's intent.

The tables present in the document, notably the comprehensive 'Post-Matthew Ground Truth Locations' data table (from PDF page 6) and the coordinate/volume table (from PDF page 8), are rendered correctly in HTML. The conversion maintains distinct columns, aligned rows, and readable headers. There are no instances of overlapping text or missing cell boundaries that would impede a user's ability to interpret the data.

The rendered HTML suffers from a widespread failure to display meaningful visual content from the source PDF. Nearly all central maps, screenshots, and diagrams are missing or broken. A series of large black rectangles appears early in the document, likely indicating failed rendering of image assets. Specifically, maps from pages 5, 6, 8, and screenshots from pages 7, 9, 11 are missing or replaced by broken placeholders (e.g., the map under '2016 Post-Matthew Operations Overview' is distorted/unreadable). As this is a geographic coordination document, these maps are critical for understanding the content.
checks.b_evidence_source: The source PDF contains meaningful maps and charts on pages 5, 6, 7, 8, 9, 11, 12, 13, and 14.
checks.b_evidence_output: In the HTML, these images are either entirely absent from their respective sections or appear as non-functional black/grey rectangles.
checks.b_evidence_location: Throughout the document, starting after the 'National Coastal Mapping Program Goals' section and recurring in nearly every subsequent section.

checks.a_analysis: There is no evidence of duplicated body content such as paragraphs or tables. While logos from the presentation's template appear in multiple sections, this reflects their presence on every slide in the source PDF rather than a conversion error. The navigation headings in the Table of Contents correctly mirror the body headings as intended for accessibility.
